title: Native Pi TUI invalid result retry proof
goal: Prove native Pi TUI invalid-result retry with a real daemon run. On the initial slice-worker attempt, update docs/design/evidence/tui-worker-retry-proof-2026-07-08.md with the proof boundary, commit that change, then deliberately submit a daemon-invalid complete result through submit_worker_result by omitting acceptance evidence (empty acceptance_status is enough). On the envelope retry for the same attempt, do not change files; re-emit a valid complete result for the already-committed evidence with acceptance_status for every criterion.
acceptance: A real native Pi TUI worker attempt writes a kd_tui_result_artifact via submit_worker_result whose result payload is daemon-invalid, producing preserved invalid_worker_output evidence.
The same attempt gets a bounded envelope retry that launches through native Pi TUI again, re-emits valid worker JSON for the existing committed evidence, and reaches a terminal successful outcome.

Evidence includes cockpit_worker_ready for the initial worker and the envelope retry with source_of_truth = kd_tui_result_artifact, the invalid-output artifact path, invalid_worker_output event, retry disposition, bounded retry count, and final terminal outcome.

The proof document states that terminal text, Herdr scrollback, Herdr metadata, and Pi display state were not parsed as fallback result evidence, and states what retry behavior proves and does not prove.
must_ask_if: The proof would require weakening submit_worker_result validation in production code.
The proof can only be obtained by reading terminal text as output.
The retry needs to change files instead of only re-emitting valid worker JSON for the existing committed evidence.
